_about: Reference targets for ddX-enabled PCM validation. PRODUCTION PCM PHYSICS IS FORTRAN-SIDE; these values are comparison targets only and never feed the SCF. See docs/solvent_pcm_literature_benchmarks.md. Two categories: 'literature_value' (transcribed from a paper with exact protocol) and 'trusted_reference_regression' (generated by ddX/pyddx or another trusted code with the exact same protocol). A row is a real pass/fail gate only when reference_value is non-null and status is 'verified'; 'pending_reference' rows are skipped even when ddX is available.

trusted_reference_regressions.description: ddPCM electrostatic solvation energy of a fixed 12-site point-charge model (no QM SCF). Validates the OpenQP<->ddX adapter numerics (model build -> solve -> esolv) only.
trusted_reference_regressions.source: source/solvent_ddx_adapter.c::oqp_ddx_run_point_charge_smoke (value asserted in-source within 1e-6 at line ~181); printed as 'energy=<float>' by tests/ddx_adapter_smoke.c
trusted_reference_regressions.validates: ddX model allocation, electrostatics, forward/adjoint solve, and esolv numerics through the OpenQP-owned C adapter
trusted_reference_regressions.does_not_validate: QM SCF coupling -- electronic contribution to psi, q_cav sign/scale into the Fock operator, phi_cav-from-density convention, and e_pcm vs 0.5*Tr[D.V] bookkeeping all remain open and require the Tier-2 QM benchmarks below

benchmarks.reference_source: pending: no exact-protocol H2O ddPCM literature_value available to transcribe; generate a trusted_reference_regression via pyddx with identical geometry/radii/discretization once ddX is built
pending: add only after H2O is clean; generate via pyddx with identical protocol
pending: low-polarity check, generate via pyddx with identical protocol
